facts.fact: Normal blood pressure is generally considered below 120/80 mmHg.
Reducing sodium intake to under 2,300mg per day can help manage blood pressure.
Regular moderate exercise can lower systolic blood pressure by approximately 4-9 mmHg.
Consistent medication adherence is one of the strongest predictors of blood pressure control.
Home blood pressure monitoring, taken at the same time daily, gives a more reliable picture than a single clinic reading.

blood_pressure_systolic.explanation: Systolic BP above 140 mmHg is classified as hypertension stage 2 and warrants medical attention.

blood_pressure_diastolic.explanation: Diastolic BP above 90 mmHg is classified as hypertension stage 2 and warrants medical attention.
